title: Threads, Atomics, Data Races, and Safe Reclamation · Lifetime Workshop
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working threaded lifetime and reclamation proof while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of threaded lifetime and reclamation proof while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.
Select atomic operations and memory order from a stated synchronization proof instead of folklore.
Publish fully initialized objects with ordering and retain their lifetime until every reader is finished.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Data Race Undefined
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 8124. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify conflicting non-atomic accesses without happens-before ordering as undefined behavior.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Threadsanitizer Limit case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Threadsanitizer Limit condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Threadsanitizer Limit
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Threadsanitizer Limit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 7933. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Threadsanitizer Limit.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Threadsanitizer Limit, keep Threadsanitizer Limit connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Threadsanitizer Limit, name the size, bounds, object, lifetime, owner, borrow, allocation, cleanup, representation, ABI, thread, and transfer boundary, Threadsanitizer Limit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Threadsanitizer Limit, trace this concrete input and predict size arithmetic, pointer range, lifetime transition, allocation result, cleanup order, printed output, and native evidence still missing before reviewing it: A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 7933. Produce bounded C evidence to model publication and reclamation state without claiming thread proof; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ThreadSanitizer on supported configurations while preserving stress, model, code-review, and platform evidence.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Threadsanitizer Limit, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: A clean ThreadSanitizer run proves all schedules race-free.
